title: 10 best herbal creams for high blood pressure

remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Ginkgo biloba extract contains antioxidants that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to high blood pressure.
Vasodilation: The plant's active compounds may help relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, which could potentially lower blood pressure.
Cardiovascular protection: Ginkgo biloba has been shown to have antiplatelet and antithrombotic effects, which may reduce the risk of cardiovascular events such as heart attacks and strokes.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, creams helps with high blood pressure because of its ability to improve blood flow and lower blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in Ginkgo biloba help to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, which can help to lower systolic and diastolic blood pressure. Regular use of Ginkgo biloba creams may also help to improve cardiovascular health and reduce the risk of heart disease associated with high blood pressure. This natural remedy promotes a healthy balance of blood flow and blood pressure.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help with high blood pressure by improving blood vessel function, reducing blood vessel constriction, and increasing nitric oxide production to promote vasodilation.
Bilobalide: A triterpene lactone, bilobalide has been found to help with high blood pressure by inhibiting platelet aggregation, improving blood vessel function, and reducing oxidative stress.

Quercetin: A type of flavonoid, quercetin helps with high blood pressure by reducing inflammation, improving blood vessel function, and inhibiting the activity of angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE), a key regulator of blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Leaves: These are the most commonly used part due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which help to improve blood flow and lower blood pressure.
Seeds: The seeds of the Ginkgo biloba tree contain bilobalide, a compound that has been shown to help relax blood vessels and improve blood circulation, which can help with high blood pressure.
Barks: The barks of the Ginkgo biloba tree contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which may help to improve blood flow and lower blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels and improving circulation.
remedy_recipe: Measure 250 grams of shea butter and 150 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Combine 10 grams of ginkgo biloba extract powder and 5 grams of beeswax in a separate bowl.
Heat the shea butter and coconut oil mixture to 160°f in 10 minutes then whisk until smooth.
Add the ginkgo biloba extract powder and beeswax mixture to the heated mixture and stir until combined.
Pour the mixture into a silicone mold and let set at room temperature for 20 minutes.

remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Hawthorn cream contains flavonoids, which have been shown to improve blood vessel function and increase vasodilation, helping to lower blood pressure.
Oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs): OPCs in hawthorn cream have antioxidant properties that help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to high blood pressure.

Cardiac glycosides: Hawthorn cream contains cardiac glycosides, which can help regulate heart rhythm and reduce the workload of the heart, both of which can help alleviate symptoms of high blood pressure.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Crataegus monogyna, also known as hawthorn, creams helps with high blood pressure because of its unique ability to dilate blood vessels and improve circulation. The flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins present in hawthorn cream have been shown to relax blood vessel walls, allowing for smoother blood flow and reduced pressure. Additionally, hawthorn has been found to have a positive effect on the heart, enhancing its pumping efficiency and reducing inflammation, making it an effective natural remedy for managing high blood pressure.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help with high blood pressure by exerting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which contribute to improved vascular function and reduced blood pressure.

Triterpenes: They help with high blood pressure by inhibiting the activity of angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE), a key component of the renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system that regulates blood pressure.

Procyanidins: They help with high blood pressure by improving endothelial function, enhancing nitric oxide production, and promoting relaxation of blood vessels, all of which contribute to reduced blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Fruits: The fruits of Crataegus monogyna are used to make creams for high blood pressure due to their high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and vasodilatory effects.

Leaves: The leaves of Crataegus monogyna are used to make creams for high blood pressure due to their ability to reduce blood pressure and prevent cardiovascular disease through their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

Barks: The barks of Crataegus monogyna are used to make creams for high blood pressure due to their content of flavonoids and saponins, which have vasodilatory and antioxidant effects that help to reduce blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried crataegus monogyna flowers at 7:00 am when the dew is still present on the plant.
Dry the harvested flowers in a well-ventilated area at room temperature for 2 hours or until crispy.
Steep 1 tablespoon of dried crataegus monogyna flowers in 1 cup of distilled water for 10 minutes at 100°f.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the prepared plant infusion with 2 tablespoons of shea butter and 1 teaspoon of beeswax in a double boiler.
Whip the mixture until light and fluffy using an electric mixer for 5 minutes at high speed.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Herbal garlic creams contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to high blood pressure by damaging blood vessel walls and increasing blood vessel constriction.
Nitric oxide boosting: Garlic in herbal creams helps increase nitric oxide production, which dilates blood vessels, lowers blood pressure, and improves blood flow to the heart.
Vasodilation promotion: The compounds in garlic cream, such as allicin, help relax blood vessels, leading to improved vasodilation, reduced peripheral resistance, and lower blood pressure.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Allium sativum, also known as garlic, creams helps with high blood pressure because of its potent antioxidant and vasodilatory properties. The active compounds in garlic, such as allicin, help to relax and widen blood vessels, reducing resistance and allowing blood to flow more freely. This process lowers blood pressure and improves circulation, making it easier for the heart to pump blood efficiently. Regular use of Allium sativum creams may also help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, further contributing to its blood pressure-lowering effects.
remedy_constituents: S-Allyl cysteine sulfoxide (SACSO): SACSO has shown to have vasodilatory effects, helping to relax blood vessels and lower blood pressure.
Quercetin: Quercetin is a flavonoid antioxidant that helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which are contributing factors to high blood pressure.
Diallyl disulfide (DADS): DADS has been shown to inhibit the angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE), a key enzyme in the renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system, which regulates blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of garlic are used because they contain active compounds like allicin, which is believed to help lower blood pressure.
Buds: The buds of garlic are used because they are rich in flavonoids and other compounds that may help to reduce blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health.
Leaves: The leaves of garlic are used because they contain compounds that may help to relax blood vessels and improve circulation, which can help to lower blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20g of dried allium sativum rhizome and 100g of jojoba oil to create the base cream.
Grind 10g of the dried allium sativome rhizome into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Combine the ground powder with 20g of beeswax and 10g of vitamin e oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler for 10 minutes or until the beeswax melts and the mixture reaches 160°f.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool for 30 minutes before stirring in the jojoba oil and pouring into containers.

remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Herbal licorice creams contain flavonoids, which are known to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels, ultimately lowering blood pressure.

Glycyrrhizin: The main compound in licorice root, glycyrrhizin, has been shown to have a direct effect on the body's ability to regulate blood pressure by inhibiting the enzyme that converts angiotensin I to angiotensin II, a potent vasoconstrictor.

Inositol: Licorice creams often contain inositol, a compound that helps regulate blood vessel tone and function, improving circulation and reducing peripheral resistance, which can contribute to lowering blood pressure.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with high blood pressure because of its ability to inhibit the enzyme 11-beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase type 2 (11-beta-HSD2). This enzyme converts cortisone into its active form, aldosterone, which can increase blood pressure. By blocking this enzyme, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams reduce aldosterone levels and lower blood pressure. Additionally, the flavonoids and saponins in licorice root may also help to relax blood vessels and improve circulation, further contributing to its potential benefits in managing high blood pressure.
remedy_constituents: Licorice Saponins: These saponins, particularly glycyrrhizin, help with high blood pressure by inhibiting the enzyme 11β-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase type 2, which in turn reduces the production of cortisol, a hormone that can contribute to high blood pressure.

Flavonoids (e.g., Quercetin, Kaempferol): These phenolic compounds in Glycyrrhiza glabra have vasodilatory and antioxidant effects, which help to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, ultimately contributing to lower blood pressure.

Asparagine: Asparagine, an amino acid in Glycyrrhiza glabra, helps to regulate blood pressure by promoting the excretion of excess sodium and water in the urine, which can help to reduce blood volume and lower blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce blood pressure by inhibiting the enzyme 11-beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase.
Barks: They are used for their antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to relax blood vessels and reduce blood pressure.
Leaves: They are used due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to high blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Extract 500 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in a 2-liter pot of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Filter the mixture through a cheesecloth to obtain 2 liters of glycyrrhizin-rich liquid after 30 minutes of waiting time.
Mix 2% beeswax and 20% coconut oil in a double boiler to melt the mixture over 30 minutes.
Combine the glycyrrhizin-rich liquid and the melted mixture in a heat-resistant bowl and stir for 10 minutes.
Pour the mixture into 500-ml jars and store them in a cool place for 24 hours before use.

remedy_properties: Piperine: Piperine, a bioactive compound found in black pepper, has been shown to improve blood flow and lower blood pressure by dilating blood vessels and reducing vascular resistance.

Antioxidant Properties: Herbal black pepper creams containing antioxidants like vitamin E and beta-carotene help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to high blood pressure.

Anti-inflammatory Compounds: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in herbal black pepper creams, such as caryophyllene and limonene, may help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, thereby lowering blood pressure.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Piper nigrum, also known as black pepper, creams helps with high blood pressure because of its potent bioactive compounds, including piperine and beta-carboline. These compounds have been shown to exhibit vasodilatory and antioxidant properties, which help to relax blood vessels, improve blood flow, and reduce inflammation. As a result, the body's blood pressure is regulated, and the risk of cardiovascular disease is decreased. Additionally, Piper nigrum's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to prevent damage to blood vessels, further contributing to its potential benefits for high blood pressure management.
remedy_constituents: Vasodilatory flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Piper nigrum help with high blood pressure by promoting the dilation of blood vessels, which improves blood flow and reduces blood pressure.
Tannins: Tannins in Piper nigrum have a vasodilatory effect, which helps to relax blood vessels and decrease blood pressure.
Piperine: Piperine, a major alkaloid in Piper nigrum, may help with high blood pressure by inhibiting the conversion of angiotensin I to angiotensin II, a potent vasoconstrictor.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Piper nigrum seeds are commonly used due to their high content of piperine, which has vasodilatory properties that help lower blood pressure.
Leaves: The leaves of Piper nigrum are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health.
Fruits: The fruits, or berries, of Piper nigrum are used for their rich content of vitamin C and other antioxidants, which can help lower blood pressure and reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 50 grams of dried piper nigrum berries and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 5 minutes.
Combine 100 grams of beeswax and 200 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes.
Mix 10 grams of vitamin e oil and 5 grams of preservative with the beeswax and coconut oil mixture.
Add 50 grams of the piper nigrum powder to the mixture and stir well for 10 minutes.
Pour the mixture into small containers and allow it to cool and solidify for 2 hours.

remedy_properties: Vitamin C: Bell peppers are rich in vitamin C, which has been shown to help lower blood pressure by improving blood vessel function and reducing oxidative stress.
Potassium: Bell peppers contain potassium, a mineral that can help counterbalance the effects of sodium in the body and regulate blood pressure.
Fiber: The fiber content in bell peppers may also contribute to its potential benefits for high blood pressure by helping to regulate blood sugar levels and improving overall cardiovascular health.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Capsicum annuum, also known as bell pepper, creams helps with high blood pressure because it contains capsaicin, a bioactive compound that has vasodilatory properties. Capsaicin relaxes blood vessels, reducing blood pressure and improving circulation. Regular application of Capsicum annuum creams can help to reduce systolic and diastolic blood pressure, promoting a healthy cardiovascular system. Quercetin: Quercetin, a flavonoid, is beneficial for lowering blood pressure by reducing inflammation and improving endothelial function, which helps in maintaining a healthy blood vessel tone.

Catechol: Catechol, a phenolic compound, is involved in reducing blood pressure by inhibiting the production of angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE), a key component in the renin-angiotensin system that regulates blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Fruits: They are used due to their high content of capsaicinoids, which may help lower blood pressure by improving blood flow and reducing inflammation.
Leaves: They are used as they contain various bioactive compounds that may help reduce blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health.
Seeds: They are used as they contain capsaicinoids and other bioactive compounds that may help lower blood pressure and improve overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature red capsicum annuum fruits when they are fully ripe and the skin is tender.
Extract the juice from the capsicum annuum fruits using a juicer or blender with a fine mesh strainer.
Combine 200ml of the extracted capsicum annuum juice with 20g of beeswax and 10g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally to obtain a smooth and creamy texture.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken completely before transferring it to a clean and sterile container.

remedy_properties: Cardiotonic effects: Burdock cream's cardiotonic properties help to strengthen and regulate heart function, which can help to reduce blood pressure by improving cardiac efficiency.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Burdock cream's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help to protect the cardiovascular system from oxidative stress and inflammation, which are major contributors to high blood pressure.

remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: Alkaloids in Arctium lappa, such as arctigenin, have vasodilatory properties that help to relax blood vessels and lower blood pressure.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids present in Arctium lappa, such as caffeic acid and ferulic acid, exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce blood pressure by improving blood vessel function and reducing oxidative stress.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used because they contain inulin and other compounds with potential anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties.
Seeds: The seeds are used because they are rich in flavonoids and other compounds with antioxidant and cardiovascular-protective effects.
Leaves: The leaves are used because they contain flavonoids and other compounds with anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties that may help alleviate high blood pressure symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 roots of the plant with thick taproots and leaves that resemble burdock leaves.
Dry the roots in a warm place for 7 to 10 days to reduce moisture content.
Grind 10 grams of dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill.
Mix the powder with 50 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes to 1 hour, stirring occasionally until smooth and creamy consistency.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginseng creams contain antioxidants that help protect the body from oxidative stress, which can contribute to high blood pressure by damaging blood vessels and increasing inflammation.

Vasodilatory effects: The active compounds in ginseng creams, such as ginsenosides, can help relax blood vessel walls, improving blood flow and reducing blood pressure by increasing vasodilation (widening of blood vessels).

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginseng creams may help reduce inflammation in the body, which is a known risk factor for high blood pressure, by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory molecules and promoting the production of anti-inflammatory ones.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng, also known as ginseng, creams helps with high blood pressure because of its ability to improve cardiovascular health by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow. The active compounds in ginseng, such as ginsenosides, have been shown to have a vasodilatory effect, which helps to relax blood vessels and lower blood pressure. Additionally, ginseng creams may also help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for high blood pressure. This makes it a potential natural remedy for managing high blood pressure.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: These steroid saponins help with high blood pressure by acting as vasodilators, relaxing blood vessels and improving blood flow, which in turn lowers blood pressure.

Saponins: Specifically, a type of saponin called oleanolic acid, found in Panax ginseng, helps with high blood pressure by inhibiting the activity of the enzyme angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE), which constricts blood vessels and increases blood pressure.

Terpenoids: Ginsenoside and other terpenoids in Panax ginseng have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect blood vessels from damage and reduce blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: High in ginsenosides, which have been shown to have a positive effect on blood pressure and cardiovascular health.
Rhyzomes: Also rich in ginsenosides, contributing to their potential benefits in managing high blood pressure.
Stems: Although less commonly used, some studies suggest that extracts from the stems of Panax ginseng may also possess compounds with cardiovascular benefits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 panax ginseng roots from a reputable supplier or grow your own in a controlled environment.
Steam the roots for 2-3 hours to enhance the bioavailability of the active compounds in a steam distiller.
Infuse the steamed roots in a carrier oil such as coconut or jojoba oil in a 1:10 ratio.
Mix the infused oil with a natural emulsifier like beeswax or candelilla wax in a 1:1 ratio.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify before transferring it to a container for storage.

remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Ashwagandha helps to adapt the body's response to stress, reducing cortisol levels and promoting relaxation, which can contribute to lowering blood pressure.

Antioxidant activity: Ashwagandha has antioxidant properties that help to neutralize free radicals in the body, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, both of which are associated with high blood pressure.

Cardioprotective compounds: Ashwagandha contains compounds like withanolides, which have been shown to improve cardiovascular health by reducing blood lipid levels, platelet aggregation, and vasopressor activity, all of which can help regulate blood pressure.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Withania somnifera, also known as ashwagandha, creams helps with high blood pressure because it contains adaptogenic compounds that help the body adapt to stress. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties help relax blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing pressure. Additionally, ashwagandha's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress, a contributing factor to high blood pressure. The cream's ability to lower cortisol levels, a hormone linked to stress, also helps alleviate hypertension, promoting overall cardiovascular health and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Withanone: Withanone has been found to have vasodilatory properties, which help to relax blood vessels and reduce blood pressure.

Withanolides: Withanolides, a class of steroidal lactones, have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with high blood pressure.

Withaferin A: Withaferin A has been found to have anti-hypertensive effects by inhibiting the activity of angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE), a key component in the renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system that regulates blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in bioactive compounds that help in lowering blood pressure by reducing stress and anxiety.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and other beneficial compounds, they contribute to the overall cardiovascular benefits and help regulate blood pressure.
Seeds: They are a good source of alkaloids and other nutrients that help in reducing blood pressure by improving cardiovascular health and reducing stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200 grams of withania somnifera roots and clean them thoroughly to remove dirt and debris.
Dry the cleaned roots in a shaded area for 7-10 days or until they reach a moisture level of 10 percent.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 100 grams of the powder with 50 grams of coconut oil and 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler for 10-15 minutes or until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedy_properties: Viscoelastic properties: Chinese Rhubarb creams have viscoelastic properties that help to increase blood viscosity, which can improve blood flow and reduce blood pressure by reducing peripheral resistance.

Astringent and antispasmodic effects: The astringent and antispasmodic properties of Chinese Rhubarb creams help to relax blood vessels, reducing the tone of blood vessel walls and improving blood flow, thereby lowering blood pressure.

Diuretic and anti-inflammatory effects: The diuretic properties of Chinese Rhubarb creams can help to increase urine production and reduce fluid retention, which can alleviate symptoms of high blood pressure such as swelling and bloating. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory effects may also help to reduce inflammation in blood vessels, which is a contributing factor to hypertension.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rheum palmatum, also known as Chinese rhubarb, creams helps with high blood pressure because it contains anthraquinone compounds that have been shown to have a vasodilatory effect. This helps to widen blood vessels and improve blood flow, reducing the pressure inside the blood vessels. Additionally, Rheum palmatum's antispasmodic properties help to relax the muscles surrounding blood vessels, further contributing to a decrease in blood pressure. By reducing blood pressure, Rheum palmatum creams can help to alleviate symptoms of high blood pressure and promote overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds help lower blood pressure by acting as vasodilators, which means they relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, thereby reducing blood pressure.
Saponins: Saponins in Rheum palmatum have been found to have a diuretic effect, which helps remove excess fluid from the body and reduce blood pressure.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids in Rheum palmatum have been shown to have anti-hypertensive properties, which help to lower blood pressure by inhibiting the activity of certain enzymes that contribute to high blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Barks: Used for their anthraquinone and saponin content, which can help relax blood vessels and reduce blood pressure.
Roots: Used due to their anthraquinone content, which can help reduce blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels and improving blood circulation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rheum palmatum roots weighing approximately 200 grams with a clean tool for later use.
Dry the harvested roots in a well-ventilated area at 50 degrees celsius for 24 hours to remove excess moisture.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds to ensure uniform texture.
Mix 50 grams of the ground root powder with 100 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 30 minutes at 60 degrees celsius while stirring occasionally until the beeswax melts and the mixture thickens.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldthread creams helps with high blood pressure because of its unique combination of antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and vasodilators. The goldthread plant contains a powerful compound called berberine, which has been shown to help lower blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels and improving blood flow. Additionally, the cream's emollient properties can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and stress that can exacerbate high blood pressure. By addressing both internal and external factors, goldthread creams provide a holistic approach to managing high blood pressure.
remedy_properties: Cortex Coptidis: Cortex Coptidis, also known as Goldthread (Coptis chinensis), has been shown to have a vasodilating effect, which can help to relax and widen blood vessels, thereby reducing blood pressure.

Berberine: Berberine, a bioactive compound found in Goldthread, has been demonstrated to exhibit anti-hypertensive properties by inhibiting the activity of angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE), a key player in regulating blood pressure.

Polyphenols: The polyphenolic compounds present in Goldthread creams have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with hypertension, thereby supporting overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain berberine, a compound that helps lower blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels and improving circulation.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes have been traditionally used to treat hypertension due to their ability to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health.
remedy_recipe: Gather 30g of dried coptis chinensis root and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered coptis chinensis with 200ml of glycerin and 100ml of distilled water in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the mixture reaches a temperature of 60°c.
Allow the mixture to cool to room temperature before adding 20g of beeswax and 5g of vitamin e oil, blending until smooth and creamy.
Pour the final cream into sterilized jars and store in a refrigerator at 2-8°c for up to 6 months or freeze for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red shiso creams helps with high blood pressure because they contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds that have been shown to exhibit vasodilatory properties, which can help to relax and widen blood vessels. This natural mechanism can lead to a reduction in blood pressure levels, making it an effective herbal remedy for individuals struggling with hypertension. remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Red shiso's antioxidant compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels, which can contribute to hypertension by promoting vessel relaxation and reducing blood pressure.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Red shiso's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, can help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels and cardiovascular system, which is a known risk factor for high blood pressure.

Vasodilatory properties: The active compounds in red shiso, including cinnamic acid, can help relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, leading to decreased blood pressure and reduced risk of hypertension-related complications.

Rhyzomes: Red shiso rhyzomes contain bioactive compounds that help to improve cardiovascular health by reducing inflammation and improving circulation, contributing to a reduction in blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Gather required ingredients including lithospermum erythrorhizon root powder 1000mg, coconut oil 200ml and beeswax pellets 50g.
Combine lithospermum erythrorhizon root powder and coconut oil in a double boiler whisking until smooth and well combined taking approximately 10 minutes.
Add beeswax pellets to the mixture stirring constantly until fully incorporated and melted requiring approximately 5 minutes more.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly for 5-7 minutes before pouring into a sterilized glass jar or container.
Allow the cream to set at room temperature for at least 2 hours before use or storage in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi creams helps with high blood pressure because they contain a potent combination of natural ingredients that work synergistically to promote cardiovascular health. The herb Brahmi, also known as Bacopa monnieri, has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to enhance cognitive function and memory, but it also exhibits significant vasodilatory properties, which help to relax blood vessels and reduce blood pressure. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds further support vascular health, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to high blood pressure.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Brahmi cream's adaptogenic properties help the body adapt to stress, reducing cortisol levels and promoting relaxation, which in turn helps lower blood pressure.
Antioxidant properties: Brahmi cream's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the blood vessels, improving vascular function and reducing blood pressure.
Vasodilatory properties: Brahmi cream's vasodilatory effects help dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing peripheral resistance, which can help lower blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots of Brahmi plant help to reduce blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels and improving circulation.
Barks: The barks of Brahmi contain flavonoids and saponins which have been shown to have a hypotensive effect, helping to lower blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh bacopa monnieri leaves and flowers in the early morning, ensuring adequate quality control.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator set at 70°f for 6-8 hours or sunlight for 3 days.
Crush the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle to release active compounds.
Mix 1 part bacopa monnieri powder with 2 parts coconut oil and 0.5 parts beeswax in a double boiler, heating until melted and smooth.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly before pouring into sterilized jars, storing in a cool dark place for at least 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Indian snakeroot creams helps with high blood pressure because they contain bioactive compounds like beraprost, which help to relax and dilate blood vessels, thereby reducing blood pressure. remedy_properties: Cardiac glycosides: These compounds help regulate heart rhythm and reduce cardiac output, thereby lowering blood pressure. This is why they're commonly used to treat arrhythmias and hypertension.
Alkaloids: Rauwolfia's alkaloids, particularly reserpine and rescinnamine, have a vasodilatory effect, causing blood vessels to relax and dilate, which helps reduce blood pressure.

Tannins: Indian Snakeroot's tannins have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help protect the cardiovascular system from damage caused by high blood pressure and other chronic conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in saponins, which help to lower blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels and improving blood flow.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain bioactive compounds that have been shown to reduce blood pressure by inhibiting the activity of enzymes involved in blood vessel constriction.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried rauvolfia serpentina root powder from a reputable supplier.
Mix the powder with 10ml of jojoba oil in a glass container for 24 hours at room temperature.
Add 5% by weight of beeswax to the mixture and heat it gently until the wax dissolves completely.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify before transferring it to sterilized containers for storage.
Package the herbal cream in tamper-evident tubes or jars with tight-fitting lids and label them with date of preparation and ingredients used.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree creams helps with high blood pressure because they contain a natural compound called agnuside, which has been shown to have a direct effect on blood vessels, causing them to relax and widen. This relaxation of blood vessels reduces resistance in the circulatory system, allowing blood to flow more easily and lowering blood pressure. Additionally, chaste tree creams may also help to reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers for high blood pressure, by promoting a sense of calm and balance in the body.
remedy_properties: Vasodilatory effects: Chasteberry cream's vasodilatory effects help to relax and widen blood vessels, reducing blood pressure and improving circulation.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in chasteberry cream help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to high blood pressure.
Adaptogenic properties: Chasteberry's adaptogenic properties allow it to help the body adapt to stress and promote balance in blood pressure regulation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and lignans, which help to relax blood vessels and reduce blood pressure.
Barks: Contain compounds like sesquiterpenes and triterpenes that can help to lower blood pressure by improving blood vessel function.
remedy_recipe: Sterilize glass jars and utensils by submerging them in boiling water for 20 minutes.
Combine 200g of coconut oil with 50g of beeswax in a double boiler, heating until the wax melts completely after 30 minutes.
Add 10g of dried vitex agnus castus herb to the melted mixture and stir well for 5 minutes.
Allow the mixture to infuse for 2 hours before straining it through cheesecloth into a clean container.
Fill the sterilized jars with the infused cream, sealing them tightly after cooling to room temperature within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Shatavari creams helps with high blood pressure because they contain natural ingredients that work to relax and dilate blood vessels, reducing resistance and allowing blood to flow more freely. This relaxation of the vascular system reduces the heart's workload, thereby decreasing blood pressure. Additionally, shatavari has been shown to have antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the body that can contribute to high blood pressure. By incorporating shatavari cream into your daily routine, you may experience a decrease in blood pressure and an overall improvement in cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Shatavari's anti-inflammatory property can help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, which is often a contributing factor to high blood pressure. This reduction can help improve blood vessel function and lower blood pressure.

Antioxidant property: Shatavari's antioxidant property can help protect against oxidative stress and damage to the cardiovascular system, which can contribute to high blood pressure. By neutralizing free radicals, Shatavari cream may help reduce blood pressure and improve overall cardiovascular health.

Cardiac tonic property: Shatavari is considered a cardiac tonic, which means it can help strengthen and tone the heart muscle, improving its function and reducing the risk of high blood pressure-related complications such as heart failure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, the roots of Shatavari are used to create creams that help lower blood pressure by reducing oxidative stress.
Rhyzomes: Containing a unique combination of compounds like saponins and alkaloids, the rhyzomes of Shatavari have been traditionally used to create topical creams that help manage blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and clean asparagus racemosus roots ensuring they are free from dirt and debris.
Grind the cleaned roots into a fine powder using a grinder or mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 200g of asparagus racemosus root powder with 1000ml of distilled water to create a decoction for 30 minutes.
Strain the decoction through cheesecloth and add 20g of coconut oil and 10g of beeswax, melting them together for 5 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for 1 hour before whipping into a creamy texture.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for high blood pressure are topical formulations containing natural herbs that help alleviate symptoms of hypertension by promoting relaxation, reducing stress, and improving cardiovascular health. The best herbal creams for high blood pressure include Ginkgo biloba, Lavandula angustifolia, Panax ginseng, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Rauvolfia serpentina, Centella asiatica, Crataegus monogyna, Astragalus membranaceus, Valeriana officinalis, and Cassia auriculata. If left untreated, high blood pressure can lead to heart disease, stroke, kidney damage, and even cognitive impairment, severely impacting daily life and quality of time with loved ones.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to high blood pressure that are treated with herbal creams are peripheral artery disease, Raynaud's disease, and venous insufficiency. Herbal creams containing ingredients like ginkgo biloba, arnica, and horse chestnut may help improve circulation, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms such as numbness, tingling, and swelling. These natural remedies can provide an alternative or complementary approach to conventional treatments for these conditions.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with high blood pressure is a blend of arnica, ginkgo biloba, and hawthorn. Arnica reduces inflammation and promotes relaxation, while ginkgo biloba improves blood flow and lowers blood pressure. Hawthorn, rich in flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins, supports heart health and improves blood vessel function. Together, these creams create a synergistic effect that promotes cardiovascular well-being and helps to alleviate symptoms of high blood pressure. Regular application can lead to improved blood pressure regulation and overall heart health.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Endocrine, metabolic & immune disorders drug targets, herbal creams derived from medicinal plants such as garlic, celery, Black Cumin, and Ginseng have been found to be effective in lowering blood pressure through various mechanisms, including direct vasorelaxant effects and indirect actions on the renin-angiotensin system and diuretic activity. The study analyzed a decade's worth of experimental and clinical investigations and identified isolated compounds such as allicin and apigenin as having a favorable therapeutic impact on blood flow. These findings suggest that herbal creams could be used as a preventive and curative agent in the management of hypertension, although further research is needed to standardize experimental study methods, investigate purified molecules, and determine the efficacy/safety ratio of these herbal creams.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams helps with high blood pressure because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that improve blood flow and reduce blood vessel constriction. The active compounds in Ginkgo biloba, such as bilobalide and ginkgolides, help to relax blood vessels, lower blood pressure, and increase the flexibility of blood vessels. This leads to better blood circulation and a reduction in blood pressure. Regular use of Ginkgo biloba creams may help alleviate high blood pressure symptoms and promote overall cardiovascular health.

Lavandula angustifolia creams helps with high blood pressure because of its unique combination of active compounds that promote relaxation and reduce stress levels. The calming properties of lavender oil have been shown to lower cortisol levels, which in turn can help to slow down the heart rate and reduce blood pressure. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of the cream may also help to improve blood vessel function, allowing blood to flow more efficiently and reducing the pressure on the cardiovascular system. This can lead to a decrease in blood pressure and a reduced risk of cardiovascular disease.

Panax ginseng creams helps with high blood pressure because it is rich in ginsenosides, compounds that have been shown to have a positive effect on cardiovascular health. Regular use of Panax ginseng creams may help to reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease and promote overall cardiovascular well-being. It may also help to improve symptoms of high blood pressure.

Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with high blood pressure because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to have a positive effect on the body's ability to manage blood pressure. This is achieved by inhibiting the enzyme 11-beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase, which converts cortisol into a more potent form that can contribute to high blood pressure. Additionally, glycyrrhizin has been found to help relax blood vessels, thereby reducing blood pressure and promoting overall cardiovascular health. Regular use of these creams may lead to sustained blood pressure reduction.

Rauvolfia serpentina creams helps with high blood pressure because they contain the active compound reserpine, which has been shown to effectively lower blood pressure by reducing the body's production of certain neurotransmitters that cause blood vessels to constrict. By relaxing blood vessels and improving blood flow, Rauvolfia serpentina creams help to decrease blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of hypertension. This natural remedy has been used for centuries in Ayurvedic medicine to promote cardiovascular health and balance.

Centella asiatica creams helps with high blood pressure because they contain triterpenoids, which have been shown to improve blood vessel function and reduce inflammation. These compounds help relax blood vessels, allowing blood to flow more freely and reducing the pressure on the cardiovascular system. Additionally, Centella asiatica creams have antioxidant properties, which help protect against oxidative stress and promote overall cardiovascular health. As a result, regular use of Centella asiatica creams may help lower blood pressure and reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease.

Crataegus monogyna creams helps with high blood pressure because they contain bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and saponins, which have been shown to exert a vasodilatory effect, relaxing blood vessels and improving blood flow. This can help lower blood pressure by reducing peripheral resistance and enhancing the body's natural ability to regulate blood pressure. Additionally, the herb has been found to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation, further contributing to its potential blood pressure-lowering effects.

Astragalus membranaceus creams helps with high blood pressure because they contain adaptogenic properties that help regulate the body's response to stress. This herb has been shown to improve cardiovascular function by increasing blood vessel dilation and reducing inflammation. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Astragalus membranaceus also help to protect against oxidative stress and damage to blood vessels, ultimately contributing to lower blood pressure readings and improved cardiovascular health. Regular use of Astragalus membranaceus creams may also help to reduce overall blood pressure.

Valeriana officinalis creams helps with high blood pressure because they contain Valerian root extract, which is known to have a natural vasodilating effect. This means that it helps to relax and widen blood vessels, allowing blood to flow more freely and reducing the pressure on the cardiovascular system. Additionally, Valerian root has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can also help to reduce stress and anxiety that may contribute to high blood pressure. Regular use of Valeriana officinalis creams may help to lower blood pressure and promote overall cardiovascular health.

Cassia auriculata creams helps with high blood pressure because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream is derived from the Cassia auriculata plant, which contains bioactive compounds that help to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, Cassia auriculata creams help to lower blood pressure and improve overall cardiovascular health.
